nav.restartServerConfirm: Restart the server? Active connections may be briefly interrupted.
nav.restartServerFailed: Restart failed. Please check the server status.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.welcomeDescription: AI API Proxy Gateway - Route your AI requests through multiple providers with intelligent failover and load balancing.

requests.title: Requests
requests.selectAttempt: Select an attempt to view details
requests.requestNotFoundDesc: The request you're looking for doesn't exist or has been deleted.

providers.title: Providers
providers.readOnlyHint: Read-only for members. Admin access required to edit providers.
providers.refreshQuotas: Refresh quotas for all Antigravity providers
providers.refreshCodex: Refresh subscription info for all Codex providers

codex.reasoningDesc: Override the reasoning effort level for all requests to this provider
codex.serviceTierDesc: Override the service tier for all requests to this provider

providers.accountRestricted: This account has been restricted. Please check your Google Cloud account status.

supportModels.title: Supported Models
supportModels.desc: Configure which models this provider supports. If empty, all models are supported. Supports wildcards like claude-* or gemini-*.
supportModels.empty: No model filter configured. All models will be supported.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete Provider?
deleteConfirm.description: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

providers.antigravityOauthComingSoon: Antigravity OAuth authentication will be available in a future update.

antigravityTokenImport.title: Add Antigravity Account
antigravityTokenImport.chooseMethodDesc: Select how you want to connect your Antigravity account to access models.
antigravityTokenImport.oauthConnectDesc: Securely authorize via Google. Best for personal accounts.
antigravityTokenImport.manualTokenDesc: Paste your refresh token directly. Best for service accounts.
antigravityTokenImport.oauthAuthorizationDesc: We will redirect you to Google to securely authorize access to your Antigravity projects.
antigravityTokenImport.completeAuthInPopup: Please complete the authorization in the popup window
antigravityTokenImport.displayOnlyNote: Used for display purposes only. Auto-detected if valid token provided.

errors.invalidRefreshToken: Please enter a valid refresh token (starting with 1//)

codexTokenImport.title: Add Codex Account
codexTokenImport.connectDescription: Sign in with your OpenAI account or import a refresh token manually.
codexTokenImport.openaiOauthDesc: Sign in with your OpenAI account
codexTokenImport.pasteCallbackHint: You can paste the callback URL below to continue
codexTokenImport.completeSignIn: Complete the sign-in in the popup window
codexTokenImport.copyAuthUrlHint: Copy the auth URL to open in your browser, then paste the callback URL.
codexTokenImport.popupNotWorkingHint: Popup window not working? Copy the auth URL or paste the callback URL manually.
codexTokenImport.callbackNote: After signing in, copy the URL from your browser's address bar (it will show an error page, that's expected).
codexTokenImport.displayOnlyNote: Used for display purposes only. Auto-detected if valid token provided.

errors.invalidCallbackUrl: Invalid callback URL. Please paste the complete URL from the browser address bar.
errors.stateMismatch: State mismatch. Please make sure you are using the callback URL from the current OAuth session.
errors.invalidRefreshToken: Please enter a valid refresh token

kiroTokenImport.importDescription: Enter your Kiro Social refresh token to connect your account.
kiroTokenImport.credentialsDesc: Enter your Kiro Social account details

errors.bannedAccount: Cannot create provider for a banned account

projects.title: Projects
projects.noProjectsHint: Create a new project to get started
projects.name: Name
projects.slugDesc: Used in URLs and proxy paths
projects.proxyConfigDesc: Use this base URL to route requests through this project's configuration. The protocol (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini) is automatically detected.
projects.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete project "{{name}}"?

requests.title: Project Requests
requests.comingSoon: Request tracking by project coming soon
requests.comingSoonNote: This feature requires adding projectID to ProxyRequest records.

routes.title: Global Routes
routes.description: These routes apply to all projects. Project-specific routes can be configured in each project's settings.
routes.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this route?

form.modelMappingHelp: Route-level model mappings take priority over provider and global settings.

routes.usingGlobalRoutesDesc: {{client}} is currently using global routes. Toggle switch to enable project-specific routing for this client type.
routes.usingGlobalRoutesShort: This project is currently using global routes for {{client}}.
routes.addRouteToGetStarted: Add a route below to get started

console.title: Console
console.waitingForLogsHint: Real-time events from the proxy will appear here automatically.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.projectBindingHint: Changing the project will also update all requests associated with this session.
sessions.noProjectsAvailable: No projects available. Please create a project first.
sessions.timeoutWarning: If no project is selected within the timeout, the request will be rejected.
sessions.rebindProjectHint: This request was rejected or has no project binding. You can select a project to rebind.

retryConfigs.title: Retry Policy
retryConfigs.description: Configure global retry behavior for failed requests
retryConfigs.noDefaultPolicyHint: There is currently no default retry policy configured. Saving the settings below will create a new global default policy.
retryConfigs.maxRetriesDesc: Maximum number of retry attempts to make before giving up.
retryConfigs.initialIntervalDesc: Delay before the first retry attempt.
retryConfigs.backoffRateDesc: Multiplier applied to the interval after each retry.

login.title: Maxx Admin
login.description: Enter password to access the admin panel
login.descriptionMultiUser: Use account credentials for regular sign-in. If you need a new account or Passkey, switch to the matching option on the right.
login.primaryDescription: Default sign-in path. Use this first when accessing from a new or untrusted device.
login.secondaryDescription: Registration and Passkey are secondary paths. Use them only when needed.
login.usernameHint: Enter the username for an approved account
login.passwordHint: Keep password login available as a fallback even after Passkey is enabled
login.passwordRuleHint: Must satisfy all 4 rules: at least 8 characters, contains a number, contains a letter A-Z or a-z, and contains common punctuation ! @ # $ % ^ & * ? . , _ - + = / \
login.passwordFormatInvalid: Password must satisfy all 4 rules: at least 8 characters, contains a number, contains a letter A-Z or a-z, and contains common punctuation ! @ # $ % ^ & * ? . , _ - + = / \
login.passwordRuleLetter: Contains a letter A-Z or a-z
login.forgotPasswordDescription: This page does not support direct password reset. Please contact your admin or approver for help.
login.forgotPasswordTip: If your account is still pending approval, wait for approval before trying again.
login.forgotPasswordContactHint: For shared environments, contact the administrator responsible for this Maxx instance to reset or verify your account.
login.registerDescription: Register a new account (requires admin approval)
login.registerSuccess: Registration submitted, waiting for admin approval
login.registerSummaryDescription: Registration requests require admin approval before the account can sign in.
login.registerApprovalHint: Your request must be approved by an admin before the account can sign in.
login.registerUsernameHint: Use a username that admins can easily recognize
login.passkeyNotSupported: Your browser or device does not support Passkey
login.passkeyRegistrationNeedsPassword: Please enter username and password to register Passkey
login.passkeySummaryDescription: Use this only when the account already has a Passkey on this device or browser.
login.passkeyHint: If this device already has a Passkey, you can sign in with it directly. For first-time setup, sign in with account credentials first.
login.passkeyUsernameHint: Username is optional. If provided, the system will try that account first for Passkey verification.
login.passkeyUnsupportedHint: This browser or device does not support Passkey. Use account login instead.
login.passkeyManageHint: First-time setup path: sign in first, then open “Manage Passkeys” from the user menu in the bottom-right corner.

inviteCodes.title: Invite Codes
inviteCodes.description: Manage invite codes for user registration
inviteCodes.createDescription: Generate invite codes for new registrations
inviteCodes.createdDescription: Copy these codes now. They will only be shown once.

users.title: Users
users.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this user?
users.changePasswordDescription: Enter your current password and choose a new one
users.passkeyManagementDescription: View and remove registered Passkey devices.
users.passkeyFallbackHint: If a device is lost, you can still log in with password and remove old Passkeys here.
users.passkeyCloneWarning: Security warning: this Passkey may be cloned.

routingStrategies.title: Routing Strategies
routingStrategies.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this strategy?

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.description: Learn how to configure and use maxx with your AI tools
documentation.pageTabQuickStartDesc: Generate the shortest setup and get one request working first.
documentation.pageTabClientExamplesDesc: Browse full configs and request examples by client.
documentation.pageTabDiagnosticsDesc: Check the current proxy, route, and token state.
documentation.quickStartDesc: Finish onboarding in 3 steps: choose client, fill token, copy config and verify.
documentation.tokenInputPlaceholder: maxx_xxx (sample token is used if empty)
documentation.quickStartDiagnosticHint: If the request still does not work, switch to Runtime Diagnostics to inspect proxy, route, and token status.
documentation.clientExamplesDesc: Review full client configs, request formats, and token authentication notes.
documentation.diagnosticsDesc: Check key items in real time and quickly locate integration failures.
documentation.diagnosticProxyRunningHint: Proxy is not running. Start maxx service first.
documentation.diagnosticTokenAuthHint: Unable to read token auth setting. Check Settings page.
documentation.diagnosticProviderReady: At least one provider is configured
documentation.diagnosticProviderReadyHint: No provider detected. Add one in Providers page.
documentation.diagnosticRouteReady: At least one route is enabled
documentation.diagnosticRouteReadyHint: No enabled route detected. Configure and enable a route first.
documentation.diagnosticTokenFormatHint: When token auth is enabled, provide a token starting with maxx_.
documentation.diagnosticTokenRequired: Token auth is enabled; please provide a valid token.
documentation.diagnosticTokenOptional: Optional now; sample token will be used when empty.
documentation.diagnosticTokenProvided: Token is provided and format check passed.
documentation.claudeConfigDesc: Create a project in the maxx admin interface and generate an API key.
documentation.shellFunctionDesc: Add to your shell profile (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, etc.), then use claude_maxx instead of claude.
documentation.claudeTokenEnabledNote: Set ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN to a token created in the 'API Tokens' page (format: maxx_xxx). Claude Code will automatically add the x-api-key header.
documentation.claudeTokenDisabledNote: You can set ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN to any value (e.g., "dummy") or leave it empty. maxx will not validate the token.
documentation.codexConfigDesc: Configure Codex CLI to use maxx as a proxy.
documentation.authJsonDesc: Create or edit ~/.codex/auth.json and add authentication for the maxx provider:
documentation.codexTokenEnabledNote: Configure OPENAI_API_KEY in auth.json with a token created in the 'API Tokens' page (format: maxx_xxx). Codex CLI will automatically add the Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
documentation.codexTokenDisabledNote: You can set OPENAI_API_KEY in auth.json to any value (e.g., "dummy"). maxx will not validate the token.
documentation.projectProxyDesc: Use project slug as path prefix to automatically bind to the corresponding project
documentation.tokenEnabledDesc: All API requests must include a valid API Token:
documentation.tokenDisabledDesc: All API requests can be made without a token. Suitable for internal networks or testing scenarios.
documentation.tokenDisabledNote: Note: Disabling token authentication reduces security. Use with caution.
documentation.tokenManagementDesc: Go to the 'API Tokens' page to create and manage API tokens. Token format: 64-character string starting with maxx_.

settings.title: Settings
settings.enableMultiTenantUIDesc: Show workspace / tenant identifiers in the interface.
settings.forceProjectBindingDesc: When enabled, new sessions must select a project before executing requests
settings.clearAllMappingsDesc: Delete all model mappings across all providers. This action cannot be undone.
settings.modelMappingDesc: Model name mapping rules, matched in order (higher priority first). Supports wildcard *, e.g., *sonnet* matches all sonnet variants
settings.requestRetentionHoursDesc: Requests older than this will be automatically cleaned up, 0 means no cleanup
settings.sessionRetentionHoursDesc: Sessions inactive longer than this will be automatically cleaned up, 0 means no cleanup
settings.retentionValidationError: Request and session retention must be integers greater than or equal to 0. Request detail retention must be an integer greater than or equal to -1.
settings.requestDetailRetentionDesc: -1 means permanent storage, 0 means don't save details, other numbers mean retention in seconds. Only affects request/response body storage, not statistics.
settings.requestDetailRetentionSplitDesc: When enabled, successful and failed requests use independent retention durations.
settings.timezoneDesc: Timezone for statistics aggregation and dashboard date calculations
settings.backupDesc: Export or import all configuration data
settings.exportBackupDesc: Download a JSON backup file containing all configurations
settings.importBackupDesc: Restore configurations from a backup file
settings.backupContainsSensitive: Backup file contains sensitive information (API keys, etc.), keep it safe
settings.quotaSettingsDesc: Configure automatic quota refresh for Antigravity and Codex providers
settings.quotaRefreshIntervalDesc: How often to automatically refresh quotas (0 = disabled)
settings.apiTokenConcurrencyDesc: Configure the maximum number of in-flight requests allowed per API token
settings.apiTokenConcurrencyHint: When the limit is exceeded, new requests for the same token return a standard 429 rate limit response until an active request completes.
settings.routesSortNote: Auto-sort settings can be configured in the Routes page for each client type
settings.autoSortAntigravityDesc: Automatically sort Antigravity routes by Claude model reset time (earliest first) after quota refresh
settings.codexDesc: Configure automatic quota refresh and route sorting for Codex providers
settings.autoSortCodexDesc: Automatically sort Codex routes by 5H limit reset time (earliest first) after quota refresh
settings.codexRefreshNote: Codex quota refresh interval is shared with Quota Settings above
settings.pprofDesc: Go profiling tool for diagnosing CPU, memory, goroutine performance issues
settings.enablePprofDesc: Access pprof profiling interface via specified port when enabled
settings.enablePasswordProtectionDesc: Require username and password to access pprof when enabled

payloadOverrides.title: Override Rules
payloadOverrides.desc: Force request payload parameters by model and protocol before forwarding upstream. Currently Codex only.
payloadOverrides.precedenceHint: When a rule matches, settings-level overrides run after provider-level reasoning / service_tier overrides and win for the same payload paths.
payloadOverrides.empty: No override rules configured. Add a rule to force payload changes by final mapped model.
payloadOverrides.paramsHint: Use a JSON object. Keys are JSON paths (for example instructions or reasoning.effort). Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, or objects. Adapter-controlled roots like model and stream are not supported here.

errors.modelRequired: Rule #{{index}} is missing a model pattern.
errors.duplicateRule: Rule #{{index}} duplicates an earlier protocol + model pattern.
errors.paramsRequired: Rule #{{index}} params JSON cannot be empty.
errors.paramsObjectRequired: Rule #{{index}} params JSON must be an object.
errors.paramsPathsRequired: Rule #{{index}} params JSON must contain at least one JSON path.
errors.paramsPathRequired: Rule #{{index}} contains an empty JSON path.
errors.reservedPath: Rule #{{index}} uses reserved path {{path}}. Adapter-controlled roots such as model and stream cannot be overridden here.
errors.loadInvalidJson: The saved override rules are not valid JSON. Save again to replace the broken value.
errors.loadInvalidArray: The saved override rules are invalid: the top level must be an array.
errors.loadInvalidRule: The saved override rules are invalid: every array item must be an object.
errors.loadInvalidParams: The saved override rules are invalid: params must be an object.
errors.loadEmptyParams: The saved override rules are invalid: params must contain at least one JSON path.
errors.loadInvalidParamsPath: The saved override rules are invalid: params contains an empty JSON path.
errors.loadReservedPath: The saved override rules are invalid: params contains a reserved path such as model or stream.
errors.loadInvalidModels: The saved override rules are invalid: models must be a non-empty array and every name must be non-empty.
errors.loadUnsupportedProtocol: The saved override rules contain an unsupported protocol. This UI currently supports codex only.

modelMappings.title: Model Mappings
modelMappings.pageDesc: Model name mapping rules, matched in order (higher priority first). Supports wildcard *, e.g., *sonnet* matches all sonnet variants. Leave condition fields empty to match all.
modelMappings.noMappingsHint: Add a mapping or click "Reset to Preset" to load defaults
modelMappings.confirmReset: Are you sure you want to reset to preset? This will delete all custom mappings and restore defaults.
modelMappings.confirmClearAll: Are you sure you want to clear all model mappings? This action cannot be undone.

responseModelMappings.title: Response Model Mapping
responseModelMappings.pageDesc: Rewrite the model field in final Claude API responses only. Other client protocols are not affected. The left side supports wildcard matching, and the right side is a fixed response model value.

modelPrices.title: Model Prices
modelPrices.pageDesc: Configure per-model pricing for cost calculation. Prices are in USD per million tokens. Supports prefix matching (e.g., claude-sonnet-4 matches claude-sonnet-4-20250514).
modelPrices.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this price? This may affect cost calculations.
modelPrices.confirmReset: Are you sure you want to reset to default prices? This will replace all current prices with the built-in defaults.

apiTokens.title: API Tokens
apiTokens.description: Manage API tokens for proxy authentication
apiTokens.noTokensHint: Create a token to authenticate proxy requests
apiTokens.authEnabledDesc: Proxy requests require a valid API token
apiTokens.enableAuthPrompt: Enable API Token Authentication to require valid tokens for proxy requests. This adds an extra layer of security by ensuring only authorized clients can access the proxy.

createDialog.title: Create New API Token
createDialog.description: Create a token to authenticate proxy requests.

editDialog.title: Edit API Token
editDialog.description: Update the token settings. The token value cannot be changed.

deleteDialog.title: Delete API Token
deleteDialog.description: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone. Any applications using this token will no longer be able to authenticate.

newTokenDialog.title: Token Created Successfully
newTokenDialog.description: Copy your new API token now.
newTokenDialog.warning: This token will only be shown once. Make sure to copy it now.

projectDialog.title: Select Project
projectDialog.description: Choose a project to limit this token's access.

codexConfigDialog.title: Codex Config Generator
codexConfigDialog.description: Generate global proxy config from token "{{name}}".

checksHint.proxy: Proxy address is unavailable, using localhost:9880 as fallback.
checksHint.tokenEnabled: This token is disabled. Enable it before using the copied config.
checksHint.tokenExpiry: This token has expired. Replace it with a valid token.

reasons.serverErrorDesc: Upstream server returned 5xx error, system automatically entered cooldown protection
reasons.networkErrorDesc: Unable to connect to upstream server, possibly network failure or server downtime
reasons.quotaExhaustedDesc: API quota exhausted, waiting for quota reset
reasons.rateLimitExceededDesc: Request rate exceeded limit, triggered rate protection
reasons.authFailureDesc: API key is invalid, expired, or account is suspended
reasons.modelUnavailableDesc: Model not found or access denied for this model

provider.forceThawWarning: Force thawing may cause requests to fail again if the root cause is not resolved.
provider.configureDescription: Set up your custom provider connection
provider.disguiseTypeNoneDesc: Forward requests as-is. No body or header transformation.
provider.disguiseTypeClaudeCodeDesc: Inject Claude Code system prompt + claude-cli identity headers. Use this for relays that price/route by Claude Code traffic.
provider.disguiseTypeBedrockDesc: Strip Claude Code identifying headers and Bedrock-incompatible body fields (betas, cache_control.scope, tools[].custom, etc). Use this for relays whose backend is AWS Bedrock.
provider.cloakModeDesc: Controls whether the Claude Code system prompt and fake user_id are injected.
provider.cloakStrictModeDesc: Replace all system messages with the Claude Code prompt.
provider.cloakSensitiveWordsDesc: Words will be obfuscated with zero-width characters (comma or newline separated).
provider.apiKeyEdit: API Key (leave empty to keep current)
provider.apiKeyExcludedHint: This provider is set to "Do not export this provider". You can still rotate the API key here, but this provider and its configuration will not be included in provider exports or system backups.
provider.optionalUrlNote: Optional if client-specific URLs are set below.
provider.createError: Failed to create provider. Please check your connection and try again.
provider.updateError: Failed to update provider. Please check your connection and try again.
provider.disableErrorCooldownDesc: When enabled, errors won't trigger automatic cooldown. Manual freezes and explicit cooldown times still apply.
provider.excludeFromExportDesc: When enabled, this provider and its configuration will not be included in provider exports or system backups. This only affects the current provider and does not reduce the export of other data such as API tokens or system settings.

cooldown.title: Cooldown Protection
cooldown.forceThawWarning: Force thawing may cause requests to fail again if the root cause is not resolved.

modelInput.pressToUse: Press {{key}} to use custom model name

addProvider.title: Add Provider
addProvider.subtitle: Choose a service provider to get started

bedrock.name: AWS Bedrock
bedrock.description: Direct access to Claude on AWS Bedrock
bedrock.regionHint: Select the region where you enabled Bedrock model access
bedrock.modelPrefixHint: Cross-region inference prefix (default: us). Set to 'none' to disable

modelMapping.emptyHint: No model mappings configured. Add mappings to transform request models before sending to upstream.
